title: 10 best herbal essential oils for queasy stomach
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for queasy stomach are plant-based extracts that help alleviate nausea and vomiting by promoting digestion, reducing inflammation, and calming the nervous system. The best herbal essential oils for queasy stomach include Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinnamomum verum, Mentha x piperita, Cinchona officinalis, Curcuma longa, Piper nigrum, Lavandula angustifolia, and Zingiber cassumunar. If left untreated, queasy stomach can lead to dehydration, weight loss, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with queasy stomach because of its natural ability to calm digestive issues. The menthol content in peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the stomach, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate nausea. Additionally, peppermint's soothing properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making it an effective remedy for alleviating queasiness and discomfort associated with upset stomachs. When inhaled or applied topically, peppermint essential oils can quickly provide relief from queasy stomach sensations.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint essential oil helps stimulate digestive enzymes, relieving symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and nausea by increasing gut motility and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.

Anti-Inflammatory: Peppermint oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach pain caused by inflammation.

Antispasmodic: Peppermint essential oil helps relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate queasy stomach sensations, such as bloating, discomfort, and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in menthol, which helps soothe and calm digestive issues, alleviating queasy stomach symptoms.
Flowers: Peppermint flowers contain a high concentration of esters, which provide anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Wash the harvested peppermint leaves thoroughly with distilled water and gently pat dry with a clean cloth.
Steam distill 100g of washed peppermint leaves with 500ml of distilled water at 100°c for 1 hour to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting distillate through cheesecloth or coffee filter paper into a dark glass bottle to remove impurities.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use within 6 months to ensure maximum potency and effectiveness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with queasy stomach because of its natural properties that soothe digestive issues. Additionally, fennel oil's ability to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines can help to calm cramps and spasms, providing quick relief from queasy feelings. By inhaling or applying topically, fennel essential oils can provide a natural remedy for an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Fennel essential oil helps to relax and calm spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with queasy stomach.
Carminative property: Fennel oil has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce gas and bloating by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and improving digestion.

Antioxidant property: Fennel essential oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to queasiness and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of sweet and soothing compounds, making them effective in calming digestive issues and reducing nausea.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant are rich in volatile oils that can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas, providing quick relief for queasy stomachs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the morning.
Dry the harvested plant material for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam-distill the dried foeniculum vulgare plant material using a ratio of 1:5.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separation funnel after 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil through activated charcoal for 30 minutes before bottling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway essential oils helps with queasy stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce discomfort. The oil's carvone compounds also have a calming effect on the stomach, easing nausea and bloating. Additionally, caraway essential oils has been known to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, making it an effective remedy for queasy stomachs and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Caraway essential oil has carminative properties, which means it helps to expel gas from the digestive tract and relieve bloating and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.
Antispasmodic property: Caraway essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles of the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can cause nausea, cramps, and discomfort.

Digestive stimulant property: Caraway essential oil also has digestive stimulant properties, which can help to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms such as indigestion, bloating, and queasiness by improving gut motility and increasing the production of digestive enzymes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Caraway roots contain a high concentration of volatile compounds that help to soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Seeds: Caraway seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive problems, including indigestion and flatulence, making them a popular choice for calming queasy stomachs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest carum carvi seeds at full maturity then dry them thoroughly for 2-3 weeks.
Steam distill 50g of dried seeds in 500ml of water for 2-3 hours to extract oil.
Filter the resulting mixture through cheesecloth and separate essential oil from hydrosol using a separatory funnel.
Dilute the carum carvi essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba at 5% concentration.
Store the final blend in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with queasy stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce nausea. Anti-Inflammatory: Ginger essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of queasiness such as bloating, gas, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The underground storage organs of the ginger plant, where the active compounds that help alleviate nausea and vomiting are concentrated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality zingiber officinale rhizomes of medium size with intact skin.
Dry the fresh rhizomes in a warm well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or until they become brittle.
Crush the dried rhizomes into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their essential oils.
Steam distill the crushed rhizome mixture with water for 2 hours to separate the essential oil from the plant material.
Filter the resulting distillation product through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and yield a high-quality zingiber officinale essential oil.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum leaves in the early morning for maximum potency and aroma.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities and allow them to dry completely.
Steam distill the dried cinnamomum verum leaves using a steam distillation apparatus at 100-150°c for 4 hours, collecting the essential oil as it separates from the water.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth to remove any impurities and sediment, and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 24 hours before use, allowing its fragrance and therapeutic properties to develop fully.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when their aroma is most potent.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place for two weeks or use a dehydrator.
Combine one part dried chamomile flowers with three parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Steam distill the mixture in a double boiler or using a commercial distillation apparatus for four hours.
Filter and decant the resulting essential oil into dark glass bottles for storage.

Mix equal parts of the citrus x aurantium essential oil with jojoba oil in a carrier blend.

Filter the extracted essential oil using coffee filters or paper towels to remove any impurities, then store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature.
Combine 20 drops of the juniperus communis essential oil with 1 tablespoon of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil and gently massage onto the stomach area for relief from queasiness.
